rules.summary: The Liquidity Parts apply to:
In the Liquidity Parts , the following definitions shall apply:

sections.chapter_title: Application of requirements on an individual basis

rules.summary: An institution shall comply with the Liquidity Parts on an individual basis.
An institution may apply to the PRA for a permission that:
For the purpose of 2.2(b)(ii)\(ii\)"), the qualifying parent undertaking must be the immediate parent undertaking of one or mo
If more than one institution subject to the Liquidity Parts is to be included in a domestic liquidity sub-group , the PRA directs that the application for permission must be made jointly by each

sections.chapter_title: Application of requirements on a consolidated basis

rules.summary: A CRR consolidation entity shall comply with the Liquidity Parts on the basis of its consolidated situation.
For the purposes of applying the Liquidity Parts on a consolidated basis, the terms “institution”, “credit institution” and “UK parent institution” shall include a CRR consolidation entity (if it
The expression “consolidated situation” applies for the purposes of this Part as it does for the purposes of Parts Two and Three of the CRR , except that paragraphs (3) to (6) and (9) of Article 18

sections.chapter_title: Application of requirements on a sub-consolidated basis

rules.summary: An institution that is required to comply with Parts Two and Three of the CRR on a sub-consolidated basis, shall comply with the Liquidity Parts on the same basis.
A CRR consolidation entity and an institution shall set up a proper organisational structure and appropriate internal control mechanisms in order to ensure that the data required for consolidation f
A CRR consolidation entity and an institution shall ensure that a subsidiary not subject to this Part implements arrangements, processes and mechanisms to ensure proper consolidation for the purpose

Application of the Net Stable Funding Requirement to Small Domestic Deposit Takers and SDDT Consolidation Entities

rules.summary: This chapter applies only to SDDTs and SDDT consolidation entities.
An SDDT must comply with this chapter on an individual basis.
An SDDT consolidation entity must comply with this chapter on the basis of its consolidated situation and for that purpose the term firm shall be read as including an SDDT consolidation entity (if it
The NSFR provisions do not apply to a firm where the retail deposit ratio condition in 5.5 is met.
The retail deposit ratio condition is where a firm’s four quarter moving average of its retail deposit ratio was greater than or equal to 50% on:
Where a firm ceases to meet the retail deposit ratio condition in 5.5 the NSFR provisions do not apply for a period of one year beginning w
A firm must notify the PRA without delay where:
For the purpose of this chapter:
